methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $3M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Krevera is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Edward and Sebastian Schneeweiss that has raised $3M+. The valuation assessment draws from a sector peer universe of 9 private robotics firms and 1 public comparable (277810.KQ), though a sufficiently narrow peer set at identical stage and valuation tier was not available for direct median benchmarking.

Stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable approach, using the $3M total raised as a proxy for current enterprise value and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement in this sector. This produces an implied valuation of $7.5M. The peer-median method was not applied due to insufficient datapoints—while the robotics and manufacturing sector contains 7 same-sector comparables (including Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity), none met the filters for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation-tier density required for reliable median benchmarking.

The analysis triangulates to a range of $6M to $9M, with a median point of $8M. This single-method result carries meaningful uncertainty around stage-appropriate growth assumptions and the absence of narrow-set peer validation. Key sensitivities include the relevance of the 2.5x step-up multiplier to Krevera's specific market position and the degree to which broader robotics sector peers inform stage-transition dynamics.
